summary:
Claim: The cricothyroid muscle is the only laryngeal muscle not supplied by the recurrent laryngeal nerve.  
Evidence: - The cricothyroid receives innervation from the superior laryngeal nerve via its branch from the external carotid artery. - All other listed laryngeal muscles (cricohyoid, arytenoid, aryepiglotticus) are innervated by the recurrent laryngeal nerve.  
Answer: B

Evidence: - The cricothyroid receives innervation from the superior laryngeal nerve via its branch from the external carotid artery. - All other listed laryngeal muscles (cricohyoid, arytenoid, aryepiglotticus) are innervated by the recurrent laryngeal nerve.  
Answer: B. Answer choice: D
